title: 10 best herbal tinctures for ringworm
intro_desc: Herbal tinctures for ringworm are concentrated extracts of medicinal herbs, prepared by soaking the plant material in alcohol or glycerin. These tinctures are used to treat ringworm, a fungal infection of the skin, due to their antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties. Examples of herbal tinctures that help with ringworm include tea tree, calendula, and goldenseal. Tea tree tincture is a potent antifungal agent that kills the fungus causing ringworm, while calendula and goldenseal tinctures have anti-inflammatory properties that soothe the itching, redness, and swelling associated with the infection. By using herbal tinctures, individuals can find relief from the discomfort and embarrassment of ringworm, improving their quality of life and promoting faster healing.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow tincture helps with ringworm because it has antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties that can aid in treating the infection. The herb contains sesquiterpine lactones, flavonoids, and alkaloids, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi responsible for ringworm. Yarrow tincture can help reduce inflammation, itching, and redness associated with ringworm, while also promoting skin healing. Its astringent properties can help dry out the affected area, making it less conducive to fungal growth. Applying yarrow tincture topically can provide relief and support the body's natural healing process, helping to clear up ringworm symptoms.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Yarrow tincture contains active constituents such as flavonoids and alkaloids that help to inhibit the growth of fungi responsible for ringworm.
Anti-inflammatory properties: Yarrow tincture helps to reduce inflammation and redness associated with ringworm, providing relief from itching and discomfort.
Antimicrobial properties: Yarrow tincture contains antimicrobial compounds that help to prevent secondary infections caused by scratching or breaking the skin due to ringworm.
Astringent properties: Yarrow tincture helps to dry out the affected area, reducing the moisture that fungi need to thrive.
Wound healing properties: Yarrow tincture promotes wound healing and tissue regeneration, helping to repair damaged skin caused by ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 ounces of fresh yarrow flowers and leaves, or use 1 ounce of dried yarrow, and chop finely.
Combine herbs with 1 pint of 80 proof vodka or rum in a clean jar, ensuring herbs are fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking daily.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out excess liquid, and store tincture in a dark glass bottle.
Apply tincture to ringworm 3 times daily, using a cotton ball or dropper, allowing it to air dry.
remedy_parts: Leaves: Contain anti-fungal properties that can help treat ringworm infections.
Flowers: Rich in anti-inflammatory compounds that can soothe skin irritation caused by ringworm.
Seeds: Contain anti-fungal and anti-microbial properties that can help eliminate ringworm-causing fungi.
Stems: Contain active compounds that can help reduce itching and inflammation caused by ringworm.
Rhizomes: Contain anti-fungal and anti-inflammatory properties that can help treat and soothe ringworm symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Garlic tincture helps with ringworm because it has antifungal properties that can combat the fungus causing the infection. The active compound in garlic, ajoene, has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi responsible for ringworm. Furthermore, garlic's immune-boosting properties can help the body fight off the infection and promote healing. When applied topically, garlic tincture can provide direct and concentrated antifungal action to the affected area, making it an effective natural remedy for ringworm.
remedy_properties: Fungicidal properties: Garlic tincture contains a compound called ajoene, which has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi responsible for ringworm.
Anti-inflammatory properties: The allicin in garlic tincture helps reduce inflammation, providing relief from the itching and redness caused by ringworm.
Antimicrobial properties: The allicin in garlic tincture also has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent the spread of ringworm and promote healing.
Boosts immune system: Garlic tincture can help boost the immune system, making it easier for the body to fight off the fungal infection causing ringworm.
Promotes wound healing: The antioxidants in garlic tincture can help promote wound healing, reducing the risk of scarring and infection associated with ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of fresh garlic cloves and place in a clean glass jar, then add 2 cups of vodka or rum (80 proof) to cover the garlic.
Let the mixture sit for 2 weeks in a cool, dark place, shaking it daily.
Strain the liquid using cheesecloth into a dark glass bottle and discard the garlic.
Label the bottle with the ingredients, date, and potency (e.g. 1:2, meaning 1 part herb to 2 parts menstruum).
Apply the tincture to the affected area 3 times a day, using a cotton ball or dropper, for 2-4 weeks or until the ringworm disappears.
remedy_parts: Bulbs: Garlic bulbs are commonly used to make medicinal tinctures for ringworm due to their potent antifungal properties.
Rhyzomes: Garlic rhyzomes, also known as underground stems, contain high levels of allicin, which has been shown to be effective against various fungal infections, including ringworm.
Leaves: Garlic leaves are sometimes used in medicinal tinctures for ringworm, as they contain compounds that have antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties.
Flowers: Garlic flowers are not commonly used in medicinal tinctures for ringworm, but they do contain some compounds with antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties.
Seeds: Garlic seeds are not typically used in medicinal tinctures for ringworm, as they do not contain the same levels of active compounds as other parts of the plant.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Burdock tincture helps with ringworm because it has antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties that can effectively treat the infection. Burdock contains arctiin and arctigenin, which have been shown to inhibit the growth of various fungi, including the one responsible for ringworm. Additionally, burdock's anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with the infection. Its antioxidant and antimicrobial properties also promote skin health and boost the immune system, which can aid in the recovery process. Overall, burdock tincture can provide a natural and effective solution for treating ringworm and promoting skin healing.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Burdock tincture contains active constituents like arctiin, luteolin, and tannins that can inhibit the growth of fungi responsible for ringworm, helping to alleviate symptoms and promote skin recovery.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The presence of phenolic acids and quercetin in burdock tincture can help reduce inflammation and redness associated with ringworm, providing relief and supporting the healing process.

Antibacterial properties: Burdock tincture's antibacterial properties, due to compounds like arctiopicrin and essential oils, can help prevent secondary bacterial infections and protect the skin from further complications.

Detoxification properties: Burdock tincture supports detoxification by promoting liver function and eliminating waste, which can help boost the immune system and enhance the body's ability to fight off ringworm and other infections.

Demulcent properties: Burdock tincture contains mucilaginous compounds that create a soothing, protective film on the skin, reducing irritation and facilitating the healing of ringworm-affected areas.
remedy_recipe: Chop 2 ounces of dried burdock root into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 10 ounces of vodka or rum (80-100 proof) over the chopped burdock, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar and store in a cool, dark place for 2-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth or a fine mesh strainer, discarding the solids and transferring the liquid to a clean glass bottle.
Label the bottle with the name, date, and recommended dosage (typically 1-2 mL, three times daily) and store in a cool, dark place.
remedy_parts: Roots: Burdock roots contain anti-fungal properties that can help treat ringworm.
Rhyzomes: Burdock rhyzomes have anti-inflammatory and antibacterial properties, making them useful for treating skin conditions like ringworm.
Leaves: Burdock leaves have been used traditionally to treat skin conditions, including ringworm, due to their antimicrobial properties.
Seeds: Burdock seeds contain fatty oils that have been shown to have antifungal and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of ringworm.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold tincture helps with ringworm because it contains anti-fungal and anti-inflammatory properties that can effectively treat and soothe the skin infection caused by the fungus. The active compounds in pot marigold, such as flavonoids and carotenoids, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi responsible for ringworm, making it a natural and safe alternative to conventional treatments. Furthermore, its anti-inflammatory properties can help reduce redness, itching, and swelling associated with the infection, promoting faster healing and relief from discomfort.
remedy_properties: Anti-fungal: Pot marigold tincture has potent anti-fungal properties that help combat the fungal infection causing ringworm, due to the presence of active constituents like flavonoids and essential oils.
Anti-inflammatory: Its anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm, thanks to the active compounds like quercetin and luteolin.

Antimicrobial: Pot marigold tincture's antimicrobial properties inhibit the growth of microorganisms, preventing secondary infections and promoting healing of ringworm-affected skin, attributed to the presence of active constituents such as thymol and carvacrol.

Wound healing: The tincture promotes wound healing and tissue regeneration, helping to repair damaged skin caused by ringworm, due to the action of active constituents like triterpenoids and ascorbic acid (vitamin C).

Immune-boosting: Pot marigold tincture supports the immune system, which aids in fighting off fungal infections like ringworm, due to the presence of active constituents like alkaloids and polysaccharides.
remedy_recipe: Harvest pot marigold flowers at peak bloom, filling a clean, dry jar with roughly 2 cups of fresh flowers.
Pour 80-proof vodka over the flowers until they are completely submerged, then secure the jar with a lid and let it steep for 2-4 weeks in a cool, dark place.
Shake the jar daily to ensure proper extraction of the herb's medicinal properties.
After the steeping period, strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out the excess liquid and discarding the spent flowers.
Store the resulting tincture in a glass dropper bottle, using 10-15 drops up to three times daily to treat ringworm.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Pot marigold flowers are rich in antifungal properties, making them effective in treating ringworm.
Leaves: The leaves of the pot marigold plant have potent antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, aiding in the treatment of ringworm.
Roots: Pot marigold roots contain active compounds with antifungal and anti-inflammatory effects, making them useful for medicinal tinctures for ringworm.
Seeds: Pot marigold seeds are a source of beneficial fatty acids and antioxidants, contributing to the plant's overall skin-healing properties for ringworm.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Gotu kola tincture helps with ringworm because it has antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties that can effectively combat the fungus causing the infection. The active compounds in gotu kola, such as asiaticoside, madecassoside, and brahmoside, have been shown to inhibit the growth of various fungi, including those responsible for ringworm. Additionally, gotu kola's anti-inflammatory properties can alleviate the itching, redness, and swelling associated with ringworm, promoting faster healing and reducing the risk of scarring. Its potential to enhance wound healing and boost the immune system further supports its use in treating and preventing ringworm infections.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Gotu kola tincture has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi responsible for ringworm, such as Trichophyton species, due to its active constituents like asiaticoside and madecassoside.
Wound healing: Gotu kola's ability to promote wound healing and tissue regeneration can help reduce the severity of ringworm lesions, thanks to active compounds like asiatic acid and madecassic acid.
Anti-inflammatory effects: Gotu kola's anti-inflammatory properties can alleviate redness, itching, and swelling associated with ringworm, partly due to the presence of triterpene saponins.
Immunomodulatory actions: Gotu kola can help strengthen the immune system, allowing the body to better fight off fungal infections like ringworm, partly due to its polysaccharides.
Antipruritic effects: Gotu kola's antipruritic (anti-itching) properties can provide relief for the itchiness caused by ringworm, owing to its active constituents such as brahmoside and brahminoside.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 ounce of dried gotu kola leaves and stems into small pieces, then place them in a clean, dry glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of vodka or rum over the herbs until they are completely submerged, then secure the lid tightly.
Let the mixture steep in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the liquid through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass bottle, squeezing out any excess liquid from the herbs.
Label the bottle with the name of the tincture, dosage (e.g. 20-30 drops per day), and date, then store it in a cool, dark place for up to a year.
remedy_parts: Leaves: The leaves of gotu kola are commonly used in making medicinal tinctures for ringworm due to their antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties.
Stems: Gotu kola stems are used in tinctures for ringworm as they contain compounds that can help to inhibit the growth of fungi.
Rhizomes: The rhizomes of gotu kola are rich in triterpenoid saponins, which have been shown to have antifungal activities, making them useful in tinctures for ringworm.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Horsetail tincture helps with ringworm because it contains high levels of antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, such as silicon, selenium, and caffeic acid. These components work together to boost the immune system, reduce inflammation, and promote skin healing. Horsetail tincture also has antifungal and astringent properties, which can help to dry out and heal ringworm lesions. Additionally, horsetail is known for its antimicrobial properties, which can help to prevent the spread of ringworm and other fungal infections. By using horsetail tincture both internally and externally, individuals can help to speed up the healing process and reduce the severity of ringworm symptoms.
remedy_properties: Anti-fungal: Horsetail tincture has anti-fungal properties that help combat the fungal infection causing ringworm, due to the presence of phenolic acids and flavonoids.
Anti-inflammatory: Its anti-inflammatory properties help reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm, thanks to the presence of silica and other anti-inflammatory compounds.

Antimicrobial: Horsetail tincture's antimicrobial properties help prevent secondary infections, as it inhibits the growth of bacteria and other microorganisms, owing to the presence of phenolic acids and tannins.
Astringent: The astringent properties of horsetail tincture help dry out weeping sores and speed up the healing process, due to the presence of tannins and silica.
Collagen synthesis promotion: Horsetail tincture promotes collagen synthesis, which aids in skin repair and wound healing, owing to its silica and bioflavonoid content.
remedy_recipe: Harvest horsetail plant: Gather 2 ounces of fresh horsetail herb, ensuring it is free from pollutants and identified correctly.
Clean and chop herbs: Rinse horsetail under cold water, finely chop, and place in a clean, dry jar.
Prepare tincture solution: Combine 1 pint of 80-100 proof vodka or rum with 1 pint of distilled water in a separate container.
Submerge herbs in solution: Pour prepared tincture solution over chopped horsetail herbs, ensuring they are fully submerged, and seal the jar.
Steep for 4-6 weeks: Store jar in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking daily, then strain and store tincture in a glass container. Apply topically to affected ringworm areas as needed.
remedy_parts: Rhizomes: Horsetail rhizomes contain silica and other compounds with antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, making them effective for treating ringworm.
Leaves: Horsetail leaves are rich in antioxidants and have antifungal properties, which can help to alleviate symptoms of ringworm.
Stems: Horsetail stems contain high amounts of silica and other beneficial compounds, which can aid in the treatment of various skin conditions, including ringworm.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Fennel tincture helps with ringworm because it has antifungal and antimicrobial properties that can help to combat the fungus that causes ringworm. The active compounds in fennel, such as trans-anethole, fenchone, and estragole, have been shown to inhibit the growth of various fungi, including the one responsible for ringworm. Fennel tincture can be applied topically to the affected area to help reduce inflammation, itching, and redness, while also promoting healing and preventing the spread of the infection. Additionally, fennel has anti-inflammatory properties that can help to soothe irritated skin and alleviate discomfort.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Fennel tincture contains active constituents such as trans-anethole, which has been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi responsible for ringworm, making it an effective natural remedy for the infection.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Fennel tincture can help reduce inflammation and redness associated with ringworm, providing relief from itching and discomfort, thanks to its active compounds like flavonoids and phenolic acids.

Antimicrobial properties: The antimicrobial properties of fennel tincture, attributed to compounds like camphene and limonene, can help prevent secondary infections and promote faster healing of ringworm-affected skin.

Antioxidant properties: Fennel tincture's high antioxidant content, due to its rich supply of vitamins A and C, helps protect the skin from oxidative damage and supports the immune system, contributing to the overall management of ringworm.

Wound-healing properties: The tincture's ability to promote wound healing, attributed to its active constituents like coumarins and terpenes, can help reduce the risk of scarring and facilitate the recovery process for ringworm-infected skin.
remedy_recipe: Chop one cup of fresh fennel leaves and roots, then combine them with two cups of 80-proof vodka in a clean jar.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for two to four weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth, discarding the solid plant material and reserving the liquid.
Transfer the liquid to a clean, dark glass bottle and store it in a cool, dark place for up to a year.
Apply the tincture to the affected area three times a day, using a cotton ball or swab to dab a few drops onto the skin.
remedy_parts: Roots: The fennel roots contain compounds with antifungal properties that can help treat ringworm infections.
Seeds: Fennel seeds are rich in antioxidants and have antifungal properties, making them useful for medicinal tinctures targeting ringworm.
Leaves: The leaves of the fennel plant have antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help alleviate symptoms of ringworm.
Flowers: Fennel flowers contain compounds with antifungal and antimicrobial properties, which can be helpful in treating ringworm infections.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Licorice tincture helps with ringworm because it contains an active compound called glycyrrhizin, which has been shown to have antifungal properties. This means that it can help to inhibit the growth of the fungus that causes ringworm, helping to speed up the healing process and alleviate symptoms. Additionally, licorice tincture has anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm. Its soothing effects can provide relief and promote the overall health of the skin, making it an effective natural remedy for treating ringworm.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: Licorice tincture contains compounds such as glycyrrhizin and flavonoids that can inhibit the growth of fungi responsible for ringworm, helping to alleviate symptoms and promote healing.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The active constituents in licorice tincture, including glycyrrhizin and isoflavonoids, can help reduce inflammation and soothe skin irritation caused by ringworm, providing relief from itching and discomfort.

Immune-boosting properties: Licorice tincture has been shown to stimulate the immune system, helping the body fight off infections and promote recovery from ringworm. The active constituents responsible for this effect include glycyrrhizin, flavonoids, and various polysaccharides.

Antioxidant properties: Licorice tincture contains potent antioxidants, such as flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help protect skin cells from damage caused by free radicals, supporting the overall health and resilience of the skin affected by ringworm.

Wound-healing properties: The active constituents in licorice tincture, including glycyrrhizin and isoliquiritigenin, have been shown to promote wound healing and tissue regeneration, which can help speed up the recovery process and minimize scarring from ringworm.
remedy_recipe: Chop 1 cup of dried licorice root into small pieces and place in a clean glass jar.
Pour 2 cups of vodka or rum over the licorice root, ensuring it is fully submerged.
Seal the jar tightly and store in a cool, dark place for 4-6 weeks, shaking occasionally.
Strain the mixture using cheesecloth, squeezing out excess liquid and discarding the solid material.
Store the resulting tincture in a clean, dark glass bottle, using within 2 years. Apply topically to affected area up to 3 times daily.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the licorice plant contain compounds that have antifungal properties, making them effective for treating ringworm.
Leaves: The leaves of the licorice plant have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help soothe the itching and irritation caused by ringworm.
Seeds: The seeds of the licorice plant contain compounds that have antimicrobial properties, which can help prevent the spread of ringworm.
Bark: The bark of the licorice plant contains compounds that have antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties, making it effective for treating ringworm and relieving symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: St. John's wort tincture helps with ringworm because of its antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties. The active compounds in St. John's wort, such as hypericin and hyperforin, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi responsible for ringworm infections. Additionally, St. John's wort tincture can help reduce inflammation and itching associated with ringworm, providing relief and promoting healing. Its antimicrobial properties may also help prevent the spread of the infection to other parts of the body or to other people. Overall, St. John's wort tincture can be an effective natural remedy for treating ringworm and promoting skin health.
remedy_properties: Antifungal properties: St. John's wort tincture has antifungal properties that are important for ringworm as ringworm is a fungal infection, and the active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin and hyperforin.

Anti-inflammatory properties: St. John's wort tincture has anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce the redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm, and the active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin, hyperforin, and flavonoids.

Wound healing properties: St. John's wort tincture has wound healing properties that can help speed up the healing process of the skin affected by ringworm, and the active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin, hyperforin, and tannins.

Antimicrobial properties: St. John's wort tincture has antimicrobial properties that can help prevent secondary infections that may occur due to scratching or picking at the affected area, and the active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin, hyperforin, and flavonoids.

Analgesic properties: St. John's wort tincture has analgesic properties that can help relieve the pain and discomfort associated with ringworm, and the active constituents responsible for this property include hypericin, hyperforin, and essential oils.
remedy_recipe: Harvest St. John's Wort flowers and leaves, filling a clean jar two-thirds full, then crush them gently to release their oils.
Add 100 proof vodka to cover the crushed herbs, leaving a 1-inch space at the top of the jar.
Seal the jar tightly and store it in a cool, dark place for two to six weeks, shaking it daily.
Strain the liquid through cheesecloth into a clean, dark glass bottle, squeezing out the excess liquid from the herbs.
Label the bottle with the preparation date and store it in a cool, dark place for up to a year, using 10-15 drops of the tincture on the affected area, three times a day.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in hypericin and hyperforin, the compounds believed to have antifungal properties suitable for treating ringworm.
Leaves: Contain similar active compounds as flowers, and are often used in combination to increase the potency of the tincture.
Stems: Although less potent than flowers and leaves, stems can still contribute to the overall effectiveness of the tincture, especially when the entire aerial part is used.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender tincture helps with ringworm because it has antifungal properties that can help to combat the infection causing the skin condition. The active compounds in lavender, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have been shown to inhibit the growth of fungi responsible for ringworm. Additionally, lavender has anti-inflammatory and soothing effects, which can help to reduce redness, itching, and irritation associated with ringworm. Applying lavender tincture topically can provide relief and promote the healing process, making it a natural and effective remedy for treating ringworm.
remedy_properties: Antifungal property: English lavender tincture can help treat ringworm due to its potent antifungal properties, which are attributed to the presence of compounds such as linalool, eucalyptol, and camphor.

Anti-inflammatory property: The anti-inflammatory effects of lavender tincture can alleviate the redness, swelling, and itching associated with ringworm, thanks to the active constituents like linalyl acetate, lavandulyl acetate, and cis-ocimene.

Wound-healing property: Lavender tincture promotes wound healing, allowing the skin to recover from ringworm faster, as it contains compounds like tannins, flavonoids, and triterpenes that have astringent and antioxidant properties.

Antimicrobial property: The antimicrobial properties of English lavender tincture can help prevent secondary infections and speed up the recovery process, as it contains active constituents like linalool, terpinen-4-ol, and α-pinene.

Analgesic property: Lavender tincture can help relieve the pain and discomfort caused by ringworm, as it contains compounds like linalool, linalyl acetate, and 1,8-cineole that have pain-relieving and soothing effects.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh English lavender flowers, filling a clean, dry mason jar up to half an inch from the top.
Pour 100 proof vodka over the lavender flowers until they are fully submerged, covering the jar with a tight-fitting lid.
Store the jar in a cool, dark place for four to six weeks, shaking it gently every few days.
Strain the tincture through cheesecloth into a clean, dry jar or bottle, squeezing out any excess liquid from the lavender.
Label the jar or bottle with the contents and date, storing it in a cool, dark place for up to a year. Apply the tincture to ringworm three times a day, using a cotton ball or swab.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Rich in antifungal compounds, making them effective in treating ringworm.
Leaves: Contain high levels of therapeutic compounds, useful for alleviating symptoms of ringworm infection.
Buds: High in antifungal properties, making them an ideal component for treating ringworm.
Stems: Packed with medicinal compounds, stems help combat the fungal infection causing ringworm.

supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat ringworm with herbal tinctures is by using a combination of potent antifungal herbs, such as tea tree, lavender, and oregano. These tinctures, when used topically, can effectively combat the fungal infection causing ringworm. Apply the tincture directly to the affected area, ensuring thorough coverage. Repeat this process 2-3 times a day for optimal results. Consistency is key to eliminating the infection and preventing its return.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to ringworm that are treated with herbal tinctures are athlete's foot and jock itch, which are fungal infections like ringworm. These conditions can be treated with herbal tinctures made from plants such as calendula, goldenseal, and tea tree oil, which have antifungal and anti-inflammatory properties. These tinctures can be applied directly to the affected area to help reduce symptoms and promote healing. However, it is important to consult with a healthcare provider before using any herbal remedies to ensure safety and effectiveness.
